name: Gut Dysbiosis
description: Imbalance in gut microbiome composition characterized by loss of beneficial keystone species (Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Roseburia intestinalis, Akkermansia muciniphila) and expansion of pathobionts (Proteobacteria bloom). Associated with inflammatory skin conditions via gut-skin axis.

name: Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth (SIBO)
description: Excessive bacterial growth in the small intestine (normally relatively sterile). Strongly associated with rosacea — 46-66% of rosacea patients have SIBO vs. 5-27% of controls. Rifaximin treatment improves rosacea in 70-100% of cases.

name: Leaky Gut / Intestinal Hyperpermeability
description: Increased intestinal permeability due to tight junction disruption (claudin-1, occludin, ZO-1). Allows LPS and other bacterial antigens to translocate into systemic circulation, triggering TLR4-mediated inflammation in skin. Elevated in acne, psoriasis, AD, and rosacea cohorts.
mechanism_summary: Dysbiosis → zonulin release → tight junction disruption → LPS translocation → TLR4 activation → NF-κB → TNF-α, IL-6, IL-1β → skin inflammation

name: Insulin Resistance
description: Reduced cellular response to insulin leading to compensatory hyperinsulinemia. Drives acne via IGF-1/mTORC1 pathway, glycation (AGEs → collagen cross-linking), and androgen bioavailability (reduced SHBG). Associated with acanthosis nigricans and skin tags.

name: Cortisol Excess / HPA Axis Dysfunction
description: Chronic elevation of cortisol due to sustained HPA axis activation from psychological or physiological stress. Causes collagen breakdown (MMP-1, MMP-3 upregulation), barrier disruption, delayed wound healing, and paradoxical pro-inflammation via glucocorticoid resistance.

name: Estrogen Deficiency
description: Decline in estradiol (E2) levels, most pronounced during perimenopause and menopause. Estrogen drives collagen synthesis (via TGF-β1), hyaluronic acid production (HAS2 upregulation), and barrier maintenance. Deficiency causes skin thinning, dryness, wrinkles, and impaired wound healing.
skin_effects: Collagen loss (2-5% per year first 5 years post-menopause)

name: Estrogen Dominance
description: Relative excess of estrogen over progesterone, often due to impaired estrogen metabolism (favoring 16α-OH estrone) or xenoestrogen exposure. Associated with melasma, hormonal acne, and increased skin sensitivity. Can occur with normal estrogen if progesterone is low.

name: Testosterone / DHT Excess
description: Elevated androgens (testosterone, DHT, DHEA-S) drive sebaceous gland hyperplasia, sebum overproduction, and follicular hyperkeratinization. Primary driver of acne, hirsutism, and androgenic alopecia. Sources: PCOS, adrenal hyperplasia, exogenous androgens.
key_markers: Elevated total testosterone (>55 ng/dL in women)

name: Thyroid Dysfunction
description: Hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism affecting skin metabolism. Hypothyroidism causes xerosis, myxedema, hair loss, and brittle nails. Hyperthyroidism causes warm/moist skin, fine hair, and heat intolerance. Both affect wound healing and skin barrier.
key_markers: TSH (elevated in hypo, suppressed in hyper)

name: Chronic Systemic Inflammation
description: Persistent low-grade inflammation characterized by elevated inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α, IL-1β, CRP) and immune dysregulation. Can stem from multiple sources (gut, obesity, autoimmune, chronic infection) and manifests in skin as accelerated aging, impaired healing, and inflammatory conditions.

name: Zinc Deficiency
description: Zinc is essential for skin health as cofactor for collagen synthesis, immune function, DNA repair, and retinol binding protein. Deficiency causes acrodermatitis enteropathica-like rash, impaired wound healing, hair loss, and worsened inflammatory skin conditions.

name: Vitamin D Deficiency
description: Vitamin D is a steroid hormone with immune-modulatory effects via VDR (vitamin D receptor) expressed in keratinocytes and immune cells. Deficiency associated with psoriasis, eczema, vitiligo, and increased susceptibility to skin infections. VDR activation suppresses Th17 and promotes Treg.

name: Omega-3 Fatty Acid Deficiency
description: Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) are precursors for anti-inflammatory resolvins and protectins. Deficiency skews toward pro-inflammatory eicosanoids from omega-6 pathway. Associated with increased inflammatory skin conditions and accelerated skin aging.

name: C. acnes Phylotype Imbalance
description: Shift from protective C. acnes phylotypes (IA2, II, III) to inflammatory IA1 strains in sebaceous follicles. IA1 produces hyaluronate lyase, CAMP factors, and pro-inflammatory porphyrins that trigger TLR2/TLR4-mediated inflammation.
key_markers: Dominance of IA1 phylotype in acne lesions (60-90% of isolates)
mechanism: IA1 strains secrete: hyaluronate lyase (tissue penetration), CAMP factors (keratinocyte cytotoxicity), porphyrins (IL-8, TNF-α via TLR2/TLR4)

name: S. aureus Colonization / Overgrowth
description: S. aureus colonization increases from 5% on healthy skin to >90% during atopic dermatitis flares. α-hemolysin (Hla) pores keratinocyte membranes triggering pyroptosis and IL-1β release. Enterotoxins act as superantigens causing massive T-cell activation.

name: Malassezia Overgrowth
description: Malassezia globosa lipases break down sebum triglycerides releasing oleic acid, which irritates and disrupts stratum corneum. Associated with seborrheic dermatitis, pityriasis versicolor, and folliculitis. Malassezia-specific IgE correlates with head/neck AD severity.
key_markers: KOH prep showing budding yeast and short hyphae
ITS sequencing showing M. globosa/M. restricta dominance

name: Demodex Mite Overgrowth
description: Demodex folliculorum (hair follicles) and D. brevis (sebaceous glands) density is 5-10× higher in papulopustular rosacea. Mite-associated Bacillus oleronius triggers TLR2-mediated inflammation. Also associated with blepharitis and demodicosis.
treatment_response: Ivermectin cream targets mites; requires sustained therapy across multiple life cycles (14-18 days each)

name: Increased Gut Permeability
description: Functional disruption of intestinal tight junctions allowing paracellular passage of antigens, LPS, and undigested proteins. Distinct from leaky gut as a measured endpoint — specifically the permeability state rather than the syndrome. Documented in acne, psoriasis, AD, and rosacea.

name: Oxidative Stress / ROS Excess
description: Imbalance between reactive oxygen species (ROS) production and antioxidant defense capacity. Damages cellular lipids, proteins, and DNA. Contributes to photoaging, melanocyte hyperactivation, barrier dysfunction, and inflammatory skin diseases.

name: Advanced Glycation End-Products (AGEs)
description: Non-enzymatic attachment of glucose to proteins forming AGEs on collagen and elastin → cross-linking → stiffness, reduced elasticity, yellowing. RAGE signaling on keratinocytes/fibroblasts activates NF-κB → IL-6, TNF-α, MMP-9. Accelerated 2-3× in diabetes and insulin resistance.
skin_effects: Reduced skin elasticity (30-50% in advanced glycation)
